channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18689] recovery-small test 57 crashes in osp and sptlrpc
item.description:
Looks like a seemingly unrelated utils patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55724 is exposing a number of crashes in osp and sptlrpc:

 [33152.842350] BUG: unable to handle kernel NULL pointer dereference at 0000000000000710

item.summary: recovery-small test 57 crashes in osp and sptlrpc

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

oh well, overnight things still come out, though at a lesser freqency I feel like and certain classe have disappeared (everythign that's inside osp_statfs in particular).

So this still looks like a beneficial set of patches, but not the silver bullet unfortunately

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57948
Subject: LU-18689 ptlrpc: Cleanup targets after proc files removal
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 94ee2810993e2894469ded2d03277330abefaeb4

I switched boilpot into "tests 55 56 57" in recovery-small for a day and managed to fix majority of crashes.

The remaining ones seems to be super fringe: debugfs would open a file (simple open would grab obd pointer from inode i_private) and by the time we get into seq_read parallel thread would free the whole subtree and obd. We do not pin obd when putting it into the inode (which sounds wrong ,but may be it's not the easiest to do it?)

this results in something like:

I checked in the corefile and entire obd_dev structure is filled with the poison pattern.

curiously so far I am only seeing thise for the only two users of the LDEBUGFS_SEQ_FOPS_RW_TYPE macro and nothing else. not sure if it's a coincidence - will see what overnight results will bring me here.

Another one almost looks like a bug in the centos7 kernel:
